senses.definition: a farming land in which corn is planted

examples.x: The cornfield stretched for acres under the summer sun.
The farmer plowed the cornfield in preparation for planting.
Children enjoyed playing hide-and-seek in the tall cornfield.
A scarecrow stood watch over the cornfield to keep birds away.
The combine harvester efficiently harvested the ripe corn from the cornfield.

senses.definition: a large piece of land used for growing sugar cane, coffee, tea, etc., particularly in a hot country

examples.x: The plantation produced large quantities of sugarcane for export.
The workers on the plantation often lived in simple housing near the fields.
A variety of crops can be cultivated on a single plantation.

senses.definition: (particularly in the western parts of the US) a male rider who looks after cattle

examples.x: The cowboy rode across the plains, guiding the cattle to new pastures.
The cowboy skillfully roped the calf during the rodeo event.
Working as a cowboy requires a strong knowledge of livestock and ranching.
During the summer, the cowboy spent long days riding under the sun.

senses.definition: all the fruit, wheat, etc. harvested during a season

examples.x: The farmer harvested a large crop of corn this year.
Pests can damage a crop before it is ready for harvest.
Farmers rotate their crops to maintain soil health and fertility.
A sudden frost can ruin an entire crop if it occurs at the wrong time.
The rice crop is usually ready for harvest in late autumn.

senses.definition: water that is held in soil, rocks, etc. under the ground

examples.x: Many communities rely on groundwater for their drinking water supply.
Rainwater can seep into the ground and replenish groundwater sources.

senses.definition: the process by which soil and rock are gradually destroyed and removed by natural forces such as wind, water, and ice

examples.x: The Grand Canyon was formed over millions of years through the erosion of the Colorado River cutting through the rock layers.
Wind erosion in deserts can shape sand dunes into unique and intricate patterns.
Coastal erosion is a concern for communities living near shorelines, as it can lead to the loss of land and property.
Erosion can expose previously buried fossils, providing valuable insights into Earth's history.
Over time, the constant pounding of waves can contribute to the erosion of cliffs along a coastline.

senses.definition: a type of chemical substance that is used for killing insects or small animals that damage food or crops

examples.x: The farmer applied pesticide to protect his crops from harmful insects.
Some people prefer organic farming methods that avoid using synthetic pesticides.
Excessive use of pesticides can harm beneficial insects and the environment.

senses.definition: related to the practice or science of farming

examples.x: Agricultural practices involve the growing of crops and raising of livestock for food production.
Many rural communities rely heavily on agricultural activities for their livelihoods.
Agricultural land is often characterized by vast fields of crops such as corn, wheat, and soybeans.
Farmers use various agricultural machinery such as tractors and combines to aid in their work.
Sustainable agricultural methods aim to minimize environmental impact while maximizing productivity.

senses.definition: safe or suitable for eating

examples.x: He found an edible mushroom in the forest.
He gifted his friend a bouquet of edible flowers, perfect for garnishing salads and drinks.
She decorated her cake with edible glitter for a touch of sparkle.

senses.definition: (of an animal, person, or plant) able to produce offspring, fruit, or seed

examples.x: She learned that some plants are more fertile in certain climates.
The fertile land near the river produces abundant harvests each year.
The fertile soil allowed the farmers to grow a variety of crops.

senses.definition: products grown or made on a farm, such as fruits, vegetables, etc.

examples.x: The grocery store had a wide selection of produce in the fresh section.
She prefers to buy organic produce from local farmers' markets.
The chef emphasized the use of seasonal produce in his dishes.
The restaurant sources its produce from nearby organic farms.
Fresh produce is essential for a healthy diet.

senses.definition: a cereal grain used as food for humans and animals and for making alcoholic beverages

examples.x: I asked my mother to prepare a refreshing barley salad for me.
The brewery sourced its barley from local farms to ensure freshness.

senses.definition: any type of plant whose pods contain seeds, such as peas and beans

examples.x: Chickpeas and lentils are common legumes used in vegetarian cooking.
The farmer rotated his crops, planting legumes to enrich the soil with nitrogen.
She enjoys a salad made with mixed greens and assorted legumes.
Legumes are a staple food in many cultures around the world due to their nutritional value.
The dietitian recommended incorporating more legumes into their meals for added protein and fiber.

senses.definition: cut and dried grass, for animals to feed on

examples.x: The farmer stacked bales of hay in the barn for the winter.
Horses enjoy eating fresh hay after a long day of work.
They used a tractor to cut the hay in the sunny meadow.
It is important to keep hay dry to prevent mold and spoilage.
The farmer sold bundles of hay at the local market to other livestock owners.

senses.definition: a legume native to East Asia, commonly used in various food products and for its oil

examples.x: Soy protein is often used in vegetarian and vegan diets for its nutritional benefits.
The soy industry plays a significant role in global agriculture.
Fermented soy products like miso and tempeh are popular in Asian cuisines for their rich flavor.

senses.definition: a type of tall tropical plant that sugar can be extracted from its stems

examples.x: The farmers grow sugar cane in large fields to produce sugar.
The sugar cane is harvested by cutting the tall stalks close to the ground.
In many tropical regions, sugar cane thrives due to the warm climate.
Processing sugar cane involves crushing the stalks to obtain the sweet juice.
Many products, such as molasses and ethanol, can be made from sugar cane.

senses.definition: to remove earth or another substance using a tool, machine, or hands

examples.x: The archaeologist used a shovel to dig for ancient artifacts.
Miners use heavy machinery to dig for valuable minerals.
The treasure hunter carefully dug for buried treasure using a metal detector.

senses.definition: to secure and connect an animal to equipment like a plow, carriage, or sled for controlled movement or work

examples.x: The farmer harnessed the horses to the plow to prepare the field for planting.
Before the carriage ride, the coachman harnessed the horses to the carriage.
The sled dogs were harnessed to the sled in preparation for the race.
They harnessed the oxen to the sled for the winter haul.
She harnessed the pony to the cart for a fun ride through town.

senses.definition: to catch fish or other animals for consumption

examples.x: They harvest the fish from the lake each summer.
The team harvests crabs from the coastal waters every fall.
It is important to harvest responsibly to maintain wildlife populations.
The fishermen harvested a large catch early in the morning.
He learned to harvest shrimp as part of his job at the seafood company.

senses.definition: to own and take care of animals

examples.x: He keeps chickens in his backyard for fresh eggs.
My grandmother kept a variety of animals on her farm, including chickens, cows, and horses.
She keeps a small herd of sheep on her farm.
The homeowners association prohibits residents from keeping any type of pet in the community.
They keep a horse on their farm.

senses.definition: to fill or pack a space with the specified items

examples.x: The cargo plane was loaded with relief supplies to be transported to the disaster-stricken area.
Jake loaded the pickup truck with firewood for the winter season.
The driver loaded the delivery van with packages for the morning delivery route.
The warehouse manager loaded the company truck with crates of goods bound for the retail stores.
Emily loaded her camper van with camping supplies and set off for a weekend in the mountains.

senses.definition: to pack something in order to sell or transport it

examples.x: In preparation for the move, they needed to package the electronics securely.
To ensure safety, he always packaged delicate items with bubble wrap.
The online retailer had to package the products before shipping to customers.
Before sending the gift, she had to package it carefully.

senses.definition: to lay things on top of each other

examples.x: The librarian piled books on the table as she sorted them into different categories.
The students piled their textbooks on the desk before the exam.
She is currently piling logs of wood in the backyard for the winter.
Can you pile the dirty laundry in the hamper for washing?
They are piling boxes in the garage for storage.

senses.definition: to remove something, such as a plant or tree, by pulling it completely out of the ground

examples.x: They uprooted the old tree to make space for a new playground.
The storm uprooted several large trees along the street.
Farmers uprooted the weeds from the fields before planting the new crops.
She carefully uprooted the flower to transplant it into a larger pot.
The bulldozer uprooted the bushes to clear the land for construction.

senses.definition: animals that are kept on a farm, such as cows, pigs, or sheep

examples.x: The farm had a variety of livestock, including cows, pigs, and sheep.
Farmers often sell their livestock at the market for profit.
Proper care and feeding are essential for healthy livestock.
During the drought, the farmer struggled to maintain his livestock.
The livestock provided the family with food and income for many years.

senses.definition: a domestic male pig that is typically used for breeding purposes

examples.x: The farmer kept several boars in separate pens for breeding with the sows.
He raised a champion boar that won first prize at the county fair.
The aggressive behavior of the boar was a challenge for the farmhands to manage.

senses.definition: the young offspring of a cow or bull, typically less than one year old

examples.x: After weaning, the calf was separated from its mother and placed in a separate pen.
The calves grazed in the pasture alongside their mothers.
The dairy farm welcomed several new calves during the calving season.
The farmer proudly showed off the newborn calf to the rest of the herd.
They carefully monitored the health and growth of each calf in the barn.

senses.definition: a domestic pig that is kept for its meat

examples.x: The farmer raised a hog for the county fair competition.
The chef prepared a delicious roast from a young hog.
The children laughed as the playful hog rolled in the mud.
The hog squealed when it was fed its favorite treats.

senses.definition: an animal that is the offspring of a male donkey and a female horse, which is particularly used to carry heavy loads

examples.x: The mule carried heavy loads up the steep mountain trail.
Farmers often use a mule for plowing fields due to its hardiness.
A mule has a calm temperament, making it easy to train.
Unlike horses, mules are usually less prone to certain health issues.

senses.definition: a type of horse that is small in size

examples.x: The children enjoyed riding the pony during their visit to the farm.
A pony can be trained for various activities, including jumping and dressage.
The pony stood calmly as the little girl brushed its mane.
The pony is known for its friendly and gentle nature.

senses.definition: a structure that is made by bees, consisting of six-sided cells where they store their honey

examples.x: The honeycomb was filled with golden honey, ready for harvesting.
The honeycomb was carefully crafted by the busy bees in the hive.
A piece of honeycomb can add a unique texture to desserts.
The bees worked tirelessly to build the honeycomb during the summer months.

senses.definition: turkeys, chickens, geese, ducks, etc. that are kept for their eggs and meat

examples.x: The farm specializes in raising poultry for both meat and egg production.
She bought fresh poultry from the local market for dinner.
Poultry farming is an important agricultural industry in many countries.
The chef prepared a delicious dish using tender poultry and seasonal vegetables.
He enjoys raising poultry in his backyard as a hobby.

senses.definition: a fast and large bird that is flightless and has long legs and a long neck, native to Africa

examples.x: The ostrich can run up to 40 miles per hour to escape predators.
An ostrich has large eyes that help it spot danger from a distance.
The farmer raised ostriches for their feathers and meat.
Unlike most birds, the ostrich cannot fly due to its heavy body.
Children were excited to see an ostrich at the zoo during their field trip.
